title: Fixation and immunohistochemical staining of Aiptasia larvae
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bhr8p","text":"This protocol is for fixation of Aiptasia (Exaiptasia diaphana) larvae and subsequent staining of microtubules, filamentous actin and nuclei. The resulting samples can be imaged under a widefield fluorescence microscope with sufficient image quality to assess basic morphological features such as size, gastric cavity and pharynx, cilia and apical tuft. For highest quality images, a confocal laser-scanning microscope can be used. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":43,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7omd5","text":"It is recommended to do all the pipetting under a stereo-microscope with a wide field of view.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"30d8t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ftbf2","text":"This protocol highly benefits from well maintained and greased pipettes (making accidental sucking up of larvae less likely and being easier on your thumb if processing lots of samples).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7mg5t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ik8u","text":"Always keep living larvae submerged in liquid, they will immediately disintegrate otherwise.  Besides that living larvae older than 1 d are very robust and can be pipetted without caution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6bsvd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3nrir","text":"Using new polystyrene plates is recommended because of their hydrophobicity so ASW forms nice round drops and larvae get pulled together by the surface tension when removing liquid. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4tept","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dhs1t","text":"Fixed larvae are pretty fragile when it comes to pipetting, try to only remove liquid during the fixation and washing steps and not to suck in larvae, the shear forces can rupture/damage them or remove the apical tuft. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4j0jq","text":"This is less of an issue once they are in viscous mounting medium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tc93","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fneia","text":"If larvae get stuck on the polystyrene, try to loosen them with a gentle stream from the pipette. Scraping them off or trying to suck them into the tip will not work or damage them.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rqfj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ea0ba","text":"If you are new to handling larvae, get some practice first and do some test fixations and washes without the staining to get a good feeling how they behave (alive as well as fixed).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":181}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"55mr1","text":"Prepare Fixatives 1 and 2 and 50 mL 1× PBST and place on ice or at 4°C at least 1 h before start.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"75ard","text":"Safety Remarks:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1660a","text":"Be sure to follow chemical safety procedures. Glutaraldehyde is acutely toxic by ingestion or inhalation, corrosive to skin and toxic to aquatic organisms. Formaldehyde is carcinogenic, acutely toxic by skin contact, inhalation, or ingestion. It is also flammable and toxic to aquatic organisms. Both chemicals can cause serious eye damage or even blindness on eye contact. Read the safety data sheets, wear nitrile gloves (do not re-use them), safety glasses and a lab coat. Dispose liquid waste containing the fixatives in a dedicated tightly closing container and dispose according to liquid chemical waste regulations. Clean up spills immediately and dispose used paper towels in accordance with solid chemical waste regulations. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":46,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":156,"length":13},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":424,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"recd","text":"Sodium azide powder and concentrated solutions are highly toxic, wear PPE and follow proper precautions and disposal guidelines.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5g3p7","text":"Equipment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cg7fh","text":"Stereo-microscope with a wide field of view and good accessibility of the object table.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a9j5k","text":"Fluorescence microscope with filter sets for DAPI, GFP and RFP (OPTIONAL Cy5). ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1d9ek","text":"For imaging of larvae with symbiotic algae, use the Alexa Fluor 594 secondary antibody for tubulin (or omit tubulin staining) and image algae in the RFP channel (there shouldn't be much spatial overlap between the stained tubulin and the algae) or better with an additional filter set for NIR, e.g., for Cy5 (downside: the emitted fluorescence is only visible with a camera but not to the human eye).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":400}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8p1u","text":"Standard microscope glass slides","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"etv38","text":"Standard square glass cover slips, 18×18mm, #1.5","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dmmgq","text":"Modeling clay","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"avc9d","text":"Siliconized 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes, e.g., Bio Plas - 4165SL","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":42,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7f4m","text":"Sterile standard 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aom67","text":"4-well plates","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8h3un","text":"We are using these plates which have a decent price compared to the Nunc ones: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":79}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aiib2","text":"CELLTREAT Scientific Products 4 Well Non-treated Plate, Sterile. Man. #: 229503, Fisher Scientific order #: 50-114-911 - ~$120/50 plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":136}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dt6gh","text":"ALTERNATIVE: Chambered glass slides or cover slips, e.g., IBIDI 80841 or 80821 with adjustment of the liquid volumes used. This helps preventing \"loss\" of fixed larvae adhering to the surface as they don't need to be transferred during the protocol. Just make sure that the materials of the chambers are compatible with the reagents used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":338}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vkjt","text":" Prepare on same day and keep at 4°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87p43","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"er8hf","text":"Hoechst 33342 stock solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bt4j","text":"10 mg/mL Hoechst 33342 in ddH2O.\nStore dark and at -20°C. 1 mL will last a long time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":29,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a8cg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"23mtd","text":"Alexa Fluor-Phalloidin:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ce26n","text":"We use either Alexa Fluor 594 Phalloidin or Alexa Fluor 488 Phalloidin depending on the fluorophore conjugated to the secondary antibody. Both are supplied as 300 U lyophilized powder which gets dissolved in 1.5 mL methanol, aliquoted and stored in the dark at -20°C. We have not observed any degradation in performance even after years of storage at −20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"duom","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"an52e","text":"Mouse anti acetylated α-Tubulin antibody:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":41}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1kp8k","text":"Monoclonal Anti-Tubulin, Acetylated antibody produced in mouse, clone 6-11B-1, ascites fluid (Sigma, T6793). Aliquot and store at -20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2e12s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bcb9u","text":"Secondary antibody (anti-mouse igG):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eu976","text":"Alexa Fluor 488 AffiniPure Goat Anti-Mouse IgG (H+L) (Jackson ImmunoResearch 115-545-003) or Alexa Fluor 594 AffiniPure Fab Fragment Goat Anti-Mouse IgG (H+L) (Jackson ImmunoResearch 115-587-003). Reconstitute with ddH2O and store aliquots at −20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":218,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"be1gd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"buffg","text":"DIY anti-fade mounting medium (aqueous, 90% glycerol; Kiernan et al., 1997):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":76}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3mrdl","text":"For 50 mL:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5tuel","text":"45 mL glycerol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33lhu","text":"5 mL 10× PBS pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"95glh","text":"250 mg N-propyl gallate (e.g., Sigma-Aldrich P3130)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b2tbm","text":"Optional: 388 µL of 200 mM NaN3 as preservative and antioxidant","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":30,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ch11n","text":"Store aliquoted at −80°C, working aliquot can be stored at −20°C for weeks. Let warm to ≤ 4°C before using.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tsg3","text":"Commercial anti-fade mounting media can also be used (at enormous costs), be sure they don't contain organic solvents and are compatible with the fluorophores used. Hard-setting media seem to severely damage the larvae. SlowFade Glass (ThermoFisher Scientific S36917) yielded the best results, other media of the SlowFade series will likely work as well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":354}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"obts","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cr1l4","text":"Simple aqueous 90% glycerol mounting medium:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ren7","text":"For 50 mL:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ql49","text":"45 mL glycerol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4coug","text":"5 mL 10× PBS pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dg8ll","text":"Optional: 388 µL of 200 mM NaN3 as preservative and antioxidant","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fes54","text":"Store at 4°C to prevent microbial growth.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fas50","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"avtmj","text":"VALAP for sealing cover slips (Chazotte et al., 2011):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":54}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5o7ei","text":"Heat (low setting of a heat plate) equal amounts of Vaseline (petroleum jelly), Lanolin and paraffin together in a glass beaker and stir well once all components have liquified to get a uniform mixture. Aliquot into small glass jars and let solidify.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ktlo","text":"Valap is basically a wax-like substance which has the advantages over other ways of sealing (for example nail-polish) that it is hydrophobic and doesn't contain any solvents which might mix with the aqueous sample and interfere with viability and fluorescence.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":260}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mchq","text":"The protocol for making the Valap for sealing the sample can be found under the following link: http://cshprotocols.cshlp.org/content/2010/12/pdb.rec12380","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":96}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":96,"length":58}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":965550,"guid":"EC118D13C482471C89738FA64D35A940","name":"BSA 20mg/mL","mol_weight":0,"sku":"#B9000","url":"https://www.neb.com/en/products/b9000-bsa-molecular-biology-grade?srsltid=AfmBOoqTI4cAjGoawPWJvsxcc9szuKuK2ZrlKSF15usJ1hUvudxWx1bS","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":2,"name":"New England Biolabs","link":"https://www.neb.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/2.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/2.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"DDCA3CAECD404C6D913131BCCD4CF5AB","url":"http://cshprotocols.cshlp.org/content/2010/12/pdb.rec12380"}}}}
steps: Add 150 µL of fresh ASW into a well of a new 4-well plate per sample. Do not use cleaned, previously used plates, the larvae will stick to it and youmight lose and/or damage a lot during the fixation procedure.
Incubate at RT for 5 min. During this time the dead larvae sink to the bottom of the well, allowing to remove excess liquid in the next step more thoroughly.
Add 250 µL of Fixative 1.

Transfer larvae with a 10-µL pipet into the wells. A 20-µL pipet which uses 10-µL XL tips is even better, e.g., Eppendorf Research Plus 2 – 20 µL, light gray, 3123000098. Pipets with 200-µL tips work in principal as well but are much more inefficient and transfer a lot of ASW with the larvae.
Remove some excess ASW (and any coarse dirt or lint if possible) with a 200-µL pipet.
Remove excess liquid and replace it with 250 µL of fresh Fixative 1.
Incubate at RT for 5 min.
Remove excess liquid and replace it with 250 µL of Fixative 2.
Incubate at 4°C for 1 h.
After the final wash step, store larvae in 500 µL PBST at 4°C until imaging or proceed to nextstep.
Remove as much liquid as possible while keeping the larvae submerged.

Do 5 washes with 500 µL of ice-cold PBST and gently shake at ~50-70 rpm (so the liquid and larvae are barely moving) on ice for 5 min at every step. Remove excess liquid with a 200-µL pipet tip after every wash step.
Remove liquid almost completely until larvae are in a minimal amount in the corner of the well. The less liquid left here, the higher the refraction index of the sample for microscopy.

Transfer larvae in 10-20 µL liquid onto glass slide, top with coverslip and small clay feet, push carefully on the clayfeet until the distance between slide and cover slip is not much more than the larval body diameter. Larvae can be concentrated by a series of pipetting steps with successively decreasing volumes.

Store the slides in the dark at 4°C until imaging. As long as the seals are tight, the slides can be stored at 4°C indefinitely but any phalloidin staining will diminish within a couple of weeks while nuclear and microtubule stains will persist over years.

Add 100 µL DIY anti-fade mounting medium or 90% glycerol in PBS (1× final strength). Volume can be increased or decreased depending on how much liquid was left in previous step. The lower the final glycerol percentage, the lower the refraction index and image quality.
Wash larvae twice with 500 µL PBST for 5 min each.

Incubate larvae in 250 µL blocking solution for 1 h at RT. Simultaneously, in a new tube, dilute anti-acetylated tubulin primary antibody 1:400 in blocking solution and keep at 4°C until the next step.
Remove excess liquid.
Remove excess liquid.
Add 250 µL mouse anti-acetylated tubulin primary antibody (1:400 in blocking solution, prepared at step 15).
Incubate overnight at 4°C for best results. ALTERNATIVELY: Incubate 1-2 h at RT.
Wash larvae 5× with 500 µL PBST for 5 min each. Use ice-cold PBST if previous step was done at 4°C.
Incubate larvae in 250 µL goat anti-mouse Alexa Fluor 488 secondary antibody (diluted 1:250 inblocking solution) for 1-2 h at RT or overnight at 4°C in the dark.
Wash larvae 5× with PBST for 5 min each. Use ice-cold PBST for the first wash if previous step was done at 4°C.
Incubate in 250 µL Phalloidin-Alexa Fluor 594 (diluted 1:40 in blocking solution, with no traces of methanol left) for 20 min at RT in the dark.
Wash larvae 5× with 500 µL PBST 5× for 5 min each.
Incubate larvae for 30-60 min in PBST with 5 µg/mL Hoechst 33342 at RT in the dark.
Wash larvae once with 500 µL PBST for 5 min. If not doing the F-actin staining in the next section, wash three times with 500 µL PBST.
Seal with VALAP (or, less ideal, clear nail polish).
Remove any liquid around the coverslip edges with a small piece of filter paper. Otherwise the glycerol will interfere with a tight seal in the next step.
Incubate overnight in the dark at 4°C with gentle agitation to help the mounting medium mix with the remaining PBST and diffuse into the larvae.
creator: Christian CR Renicke
